Rule.text: MISRA Required
Any implementation-defined behaviour on which the output of the program depends shall be documented and understood

Message.text: 0292. [I] Source file '%s' has comments containing one of the characters '$', '@' or '`'.

Rule.text: A project shall not contain unreachable code

Message.text: 1503. The function '%1s' is defined but is not used within this project.

C99 Specific Directives

A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible

Message.text: 2987. This function call produces no side effects and is redundant.

Rule.text: An external object or function shall be declared once in one and only one file

Rule.text: Controlling expressions shall not be invariant

Rule.text: MISRA Advisory
A full expression containing an increment (++) or decrement (--) operator should have no other potential side effects other than that caused by the increment or decrement operator

Message.text: 3387. A full expression containing an increment (++) or decrement (--) operator should have no potential side effects other than that caused by the increment or decrement operator.
3440. Using the value resulting from a ++ or -- operation.

Rule.text: A function parameter should not be modified

Message.text: 1338. The parameter '%1s' is being modified.

Rule.text: A function should have a single point of exit at the end

Message.text: 2889. This function has more than one 'return' path.

Rule.text: A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible

Message.text: 3673. The object addressed by the pointer parameter '%1s' is not modified and so the pointer could be of type 'pointer to const'.

Rule.text: Functions and objects should not be defined with external linkage if they are referenced in only one translation unit

Message.text: 1505. The function '%1s' is only referenced in the translation unit where it is defined.

Any implementation-defined behaviour on which the output of the program depends shall be documented and understood

Message.text: 0292. [I] Source file '%s' has comments containing one of the characters '$', '@' or '`'.

Rule.text: A project shall not contain unreachable code

Message.text: 1503. The function '%1s' is defined but is not used within this project.

Rule.text: Expressions resulting from the expansion of macro parameters shall be enclosed in parentheses

Message.text: 3432. Simple macro argument expression is not parenthesized.

Rule.text: The pointer arguments to the Standard Library functions memcpy, memmove and memcmp shall be pointers to qualified or unqualified versions of compatible types

Message.text: 1496. [I] Destination and source objects may have incompatible types.

Rule.text: The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used

Message.text: 3200. '%1s' returns a value which is not being used.

3673. The object addressed by the pointer parameter '%1s' is not modified and so the pointer could be of type 'pointer to const'.
1503. The function '%1s' is defined but is not used within this project.
1505. The function '%1s' is only referenced in the translation unit where it is defined.

Rule.text: MISRA Advisory
A full expression containing an increment (++) or decrement (--) operator should have no other potential side effects other than that caused by the increment or decrement operator

Message.text: 3440. Using the value resulting from a ++ or -- operation.

Rule.text: A function should have a single point of exit at the end

Message.text: 2889. This function has more than one 'return' path.

Rule.text: A pointer should point to a const-qualified type whenever possible

Message.text: 3673. The object addressed by the pointer parameter '%1s' is not modified and so the pointer could be of type 'pointer to const'.

Rule.text: A project should not contain unused macro definitions

Message.text: 1534. The macro '%1s' is declared but not used within this project.

Rule.text: A project should not contain unused object definitions

Message.text: 3205. The identifier '%1s' is not used and could be removed.

Rule.text: A project should not contain unused type declarations

Message.text: 3205. The identifier '%1s' is not used and could be removed.

Rule.text: An object should be declared at block scope if its identifier only appears in a single function

Message.text: 1514. The object '%1s' is only referenced by function '%2s', in the translation unit where it is defined

Rule.text: Functions and objects should not be defined with external linkage if they are referenced in only one translation unit

Message.text: 1504. The object '%1s' is only referenced in the translation unit where it is defined.
1505. The function '%1s' is only referenced in the translation unit where it is defined.
1514. The object '%1s' is only referenced by function '%2s', in the translation unit where it is defined

Rule.text: Language extensions should not be used

Message.text: 1035. [E] No macro arguments supplied for variable argument list. This is a language extension.
1036. [E] Comma before ## ignored in expansion of variadic macro. This is a language extension.
2071. [E] This attribute syntax is a language extension.

Rule.text: MISRA Required
Any implementation-defined behaviour on which the output of the program depends shall be documented and understood

Message.text: 0292. [I] Source file '%s' has comments containing one of the characters '$', '@' or '`'.
1035. [E] No macro arguments supplied for variable argument list. This is a language extension.
1036. [E] Comma before ## ignored in expansion of variadic macro. This is a language extension.
2071. [E] This attribute syntax is a language extension.

Rule.text: A compatible declaration shall be visible when an object or function with external linkage is defined

Message.text: 3408. '%1s' has external linkage and is being defined without any previous declaration.

Rule.text: A project shall not contain dead code

Message.text: 2983. This assignment is redundant. The value of this object is never subsequently used.

Rule.text: A project shall not contain unreachable code

Message.text: 1503. The function '%1s' is defined but is not used within this project.
2880. This code is unreachable.

Rule.text: An external object or function shall be declared once in one and only one file

Message.text: 3449. Multiple declarations of external object or function.
3451. The global identifier '%1s' has been declared in more than one file.

Rule.text: Controlling expressions shall not be invariant

Message.text: 2742. This 'if' controlling expression is a constant expression and its value is 'false'.

Rule.text: The pointer arguments to the Standard Library functions memcpy, memmove and memcmp shall be pointers to qualified or unqualified versions of compatible types

Message.text: 1495. [I] Destination and source objects have incompatible types.

Rule.text: The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used

Message.text: 3200. '%1s' returns a value which is not being used.

Rule.text: MISRA Required
C99 Specific Directives
Any implementation-defined behaviour on which the output of the program depends shall be documented and understood

Message.text: 0380. [L] Number of macro definitions exceeds 4095 - program does not conform strictly to ISO:C99.

Any implementation-defined behaviour on which the output of the program depends shall be documented and understood

Rule.text: MISRA Required
Any implementation-defined behaviour on which the output of the program depends shall be documented and understood

Message.text: 0292. [I] Source file '%s' has comments containing one of the characters '$', '@' or '`'.

Rule.text: A compatible declaration shall be visible when an object or function with external linkage is defined

Message.text: 3408. '%1s' has external linkage and is being defined without any previous declaration.

Rule.text: A project shall not contain dead code

Message.text: 2985. This operation is redundant. The value of the result is always that of the left-hand operand.

Rule.text: A project shall not contain unreachable code

Message.text: 1503. The function '%1s' is defined but is not used within this project.

Rule.text: Both operands of an operator in which the usual arithmetic conversions are performed shall have the same essential type category

Message.text: 1840. The %1s operand is constant, 'essentially signed' (%2s) and non-negative but will be implicitly converted to an unsigned type (%3s) in this arithmetic operation.
1851. The %1s operand is 'essentially unsigned' (%2s) but will be implicitly converted to a signed type (%3s) in this bitwise operation.

Rule.text: Operands shall not be of an inappropriate essential type.

Message.text: 4532. An expression of 'essentially signed' type (%1s) is being used as the %2s operand of this bitwise operator (%3s).
4542. A non-negative constant expression of 'essentially signed' type (%1s) is being used as the %2s operand of this bitwise operator (%3s).
4543. A non-negative constant expression of 'essentially signed' type (%1s) is being used as the left-hand operand of this shift operator (%2s).
4559. An expression of 'essentially unsigned' type (%1s) is being used as the first operand of this conditional operator (%2s).

Rule.text: The right hand operand of a shift operator shall lie in the range zero to one less than the width in bits of the essential type of the left hand operand

Message.text: 0499. Right operand of shift operator is greater than or equal to the width of the essential type of the left operand.

Rule.text: The value of an expression shall not be assigned to an object with a narrower essential type or of a different essential type category.

Message.text: 4434. A non-constant expression of 'essentially signed' type (%1s) is being converted to unsigned type, '%2s' on assignment.

Rule.text: MISRA Required
C99 Specific Directives
Any implementation-defined behaviour on which the output of the program depends shall be documented and understood

Message.text: 0380. [L] Number of macro definitions exceeds 4095 - program does not conform strictly to ISO:C99.

Rule.text: MISRA Required
C11 Specific Directives
Any implementation-defined behaviour on which the output of the program depends shall be documented and understood

Rule.text: MISRA Advisory
Identifiers in the same name space with overlapping visibility should be typographically unambiguous

Message.text: 1710. Identifiers have the same matching pattern '%1s'.

Rule.text: Identifiers that define objects or functions with internal linkage should be unique

Message.text: 1527. Object/function '%1s' with internal linkage has same identifier as another object/function with internal linkage.

Rule.text: MISRA Required
All declarations of an object or function shall use the same names and type qualifiers

Message.text: 1707. Function '%1s' is not using the same aliases.

Rule.text: An external object or function shall be declared once in one and only one file

Message.text: 1513. Identifier '%1s' with external linkage has separate non-defining declarations in more than one location.

Rule.text: An identifier with external linkage shall have exactly one external definition

Message.text: 1509. '%1s' has external linkage and has multiple definitions.
1752. The object '%1s' with external linkage is declared but not defined within this project.
1753. The function '%1s' with external linkage is declared but not defined within this project.

Rule.text: There shall be no occurrence of undefined or critical unspecified behaviour

Message.text: 1509. '%1s' has external linkage and has multiple definitions.
1509. '%1s' has external linkage and has multiple definitions.
1513. Identifier '%1s' with external linkage has separate non-defining declarations in more than one location.
1527. Object/function '%1s' with internal linkage has same identifier as another object/function with internal linkage.

1710. Identifiers have the same matching pattern '%1s'.

1753. The function '%1s' with external linkage is declared but not defined within this project.
